agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product purchase lifecycle — from cart management through checkout, payment, and post-sale refund handling. The domain encodes inventory reservation semantics, promotional pricing constraints, order immutability after payment, and time-bound refund policies.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of items a customer intends to purchase. No inventory is reserved at this stage; it is a statement of intent, not a commitment."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated by a customer to purchase cart contents. Triggers inventory reservation and begins the payment window."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-bound hold on inventory units tied to a specific checkout session. Prevents overselling during the payment window."},{"term":"Reservation Timeout","definition":"The 15-minute window after checkout begins during which payment must be completed. Expiry releases the reserved inventory."},{"term":"Promotion","definition":"A seller-initiated pricing reduction applied to a specific product. At most one may be active per product at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A buyer-initiated pricing reduction applied at order level via a code. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing on the same order."},{"term":"Order","definition":"An immutable record of a completed purchase, created upon payment confirmation. Cannot be modified after payment is confirmed."},{"term":"Payment Confirmation","definition":"The event marking successful payment processing. Transitions the order to an immutable, fulfilled state."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-day period following delivery during which a customer may request a refund."},{"term":"Listed Product","definition":"A product visible in the catalog. Visibility does not imply purchasability — zero-inventory listed products cannot be purchased."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Inventory is not reserved when items are added to the cart; reservation occurs only when checkout begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Reserving on cart-add would corrupt inventory counts system-wide for uncommitted intent, leading to phantom stock-outs. This is a data consistency constraint.","testable_assertion":"Adding an item to a cart MUST NOT decrement available inventory or create any reservation record."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"If payment is not completed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation, the inventory reservation is released.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute timeout is a business policy. It could be changed to 10 or 20 minutes based on conversion data without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A reservation that has existed for more than 15 minutes without payment confirmation MUST be released, and the reserved units MUST be returned to available inventory."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product can have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on the same product create ambiguous pricing — the system cannot determine which price to display. This is a state integrity constraint preventing corrupt/contradictory pricing.","testable_assertion":"Attempting to activate a second promotion on a product that already has an active promotion MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing on the same order. Only one discount mechanism applies.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing both creates undefined or unintended pricing that could result in below-cost sales. This is a financial integrity constraint — the system must deterministically resolve to exactly one discount path.","testable_assertion":"When a product in the order has an active promotion, applying a discount code MUST be rejected (or vice versa). The order total MUST reflect exactly zero or one discount mechanism."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification violates financial record integrity, breaks audit trails, and could desynchronize payment, fulfillment, and accounting systems. This is a hard state-transition constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to add, remove, or change line items, quantities, or pricing on an order with status 'paid' or beyond MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing purchase of zero-inventory items creates fulfillment obligations that cannot be met — this is a physical constraint. Listing is a catalog concern; purchasability is an inventory concern.","testable_assertion":"Checkout initiation MUST fail for any line item whose product has zero available inventory at reservation time."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refunds must be requested within 30 days of the delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a business policy that could be extended for VIP customers, holiday seasons, or regulatory changes without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A refund request submitted more than 30 days after the recorded delivery date MUST be rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"They view or modify the cart without initiating checkout","then":"Available inventory for those products remains unchanged and no reservation records exist"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with 3 units of Product X (5 available)","when":"Checkout begins successfully","then":"A reservation of 3 units is created and available inventory for Product X reads 2"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A checkout was initiated 15 minutes ago with no payment","when":"The reservation timeout fires","then":"The reservation is released and the reserved units are added back to available inventory"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A checkout was initiated 14 minutes ago with no payment","when":"The customer submits payment","then":"Payment is processed normally and the reservation is consumed (converted to a confirmed order)"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"Product X already has an active promotion 'Summer Sale'","when":"A seller attempts to activate a second promotion 'Flash Deal' on Product X","then":"The system rejects the second promotion with an error indicating one is already active"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer's cart contains Product X which has an active 20% promotion","when":"The customer attempts to apply discount code 'SAVE10' at checkout","then":"The system rejects the discount code and informs the customer that discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer's cart contains Product Y with no active promotion","when":"The customer applies discount code 'SAVE10' at checkout","then":"The discount code is accepted and applied to the order total"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order has been paid and confirmed","when":"The customer or an agent attempts to modify the order (add/remove items, change quantities)","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"Product X is listed in the catalog with 0 available inventory","when":"A customer attempts to initiate checkout with Product X in their cart","then":"Checkout fails with a clear message that the product is out of stock"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered 29 days ago","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An order was delivered 31 days ago","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is rejected with a message indicating the 30-day window has elapsed"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A customer initiates checkout with Products X (2 available) and Y (0 available)","when":"The system validates the cart for checkout","then":"Checkout is blocked, the zero-inventory item is identified, and no reservation is created for any item in the cart"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Two customers initiate checkout simultaneously for the last unit of a product","expected_behavior":"Exactly one checkout succeeds with a reservation; the other is rejected with an out-of-stock error. The system must enforce this atomically to prevent overselling."},{"scenario":"Inventory reaches zero due to a reservation while another customer has the same product in their cart","expected_behavior":"The cart may still display the product, but checkout initiation must fail with an out-of-stock error at reservation time."},{"scenario":"Payment is submitted at exactly the 15-minute mark","expected_behavior":"The spec says 'within 15 minutes.' The boundary must be clearly defined — recommend treating the 15-minute mark as expired (exclusive upper bound) and documenting this."},{"scenario":"A promotion ends while a customer is mid-checkout with promotional pricing","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout initiation should be honored for the duration of the reservation window. The reservation includes the pricing snapshot."},{"scenario":"Customer applies a discount code, then a promotion is activated on the product before payment","expected_behavior":"The discount code was applied first and should remain in effect. The mutual exclusion rule prevents the promotional price from also applying."},{"scenario":"Product inventory drops to zero between cart-add and checkout initiation","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation must validate current inventory and reject the checkout. The cart state is stale by design (no reservation on cart-add)."},{"scenario":"Refund requested on the exact 30th day after delivery","expected_behavior":"The spec says 'within 30 days.' Day 30 should be accepted. Recommend defining this as delivery_date + 30 calendar days, inclusive."},{"scenario":"Delivery date is disputed or not yet recorded when refund is requested","expected_behavior":"The system cannot evaluate the refund window without a delivery date. The refund request should be held pending delivery confirmation, or escalated."},{"scenario":"An order contains both promoted and non-promoted products, and a discount code is applied","expected_behavior":"Spec says discount codes cannot combine with promotional pricing per order. The entire order is blocked from using the discount code if any item has a promotion — clarification may be needed on whether this is per-line-item or per-order."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect inventory reservation logic leads to overselling (costly cancellations, reputation damage) or phantom stock-outs (lost revenue). The discount mutual-exclusion rule prevents unintended margin erosion from stacked discounts. The 30-day refund window bounds financial liability.","operational":"The 15-minute reservation timeout directly impacts checkout conversion rates — too short loses sales, too long creates artificial scarcity. Promotion uniqueness per product simplifies pricing logic and eliminates ambiguity in catalog display. Order immutability post-payment simplifies fulfillment pipelines.","user":"Cart without reservation provides a frictionless browsing experience but risks disappointment at checkout. Clear messaging on out-of-stock and discount conflicts is critical to user trust. The refund window sets clear customer expectations.","risk":"Race conditions on inventory reservation are the highest technical risk — overselling has direct financial and reputational cost. The discount code / promotion mutual exclusion boundary (per-order vs per-line-item) is ambiguous and could lead to incorrect pricing if misimplemented.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Cart experience, checkout success rate, discount clarity, refund eligibility — all directly affect satisfaction and repeat purchase behavior."},{"group":"Merchandising / Marketing","impact":"Promotion activation constraints (one-per-product) affect campaign flexibility. Discount code exclusion rules affect promotional strategy design."},{"group":"Finance / Accounting","impact":"Order immutability post-payment is critical for clean ledger reconciliation. Refund window bounds contingent liabilities. Discount stacking prevention protects margins."},{"group":"Warehouse / Fulfillment","impact":"Reservation-based inventory model determines pick accuracy. Zero-inventory purchasability block prevents unfulfillable orders from entering the pipeline."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must understand refund window enforcement, discount exclusion rules, and order immutability to set correct expectations with customers."}]},"confidence":{"level":78,"reasoning":"The spec is relatively clear and internally consistent. Most rules are unambiguous. Confidence is not higher because: (1) the discount code vs promotion exclusion scope (per-order vs per-line-item) is ambiguous, (2) boundary conditions on time windows (15 min, 30 days) need precise definition, and (3) no mention of partial checkout failure semantics (should the entire cart fail if one item is out of stock, or just that item?).","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory is not reserved at cart-add time","Order immutability after payment confirmation","One active promotion per product maximum","Refund request requires delivery within 30 days"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether discount vs promotion exclusion is per-order or per-line-item — spec says 'per order' but mixed carts create ambiguity","Exact boundary behavior at 15-minute and 30-day thresholds (inclusive vs exclusive)","Whether checkout should fail atomically for the whole cart or allow partial checkout when some items are out of stock","Price-lock semantics during the reservation window if promotions change"],"assumptions":["The 15-minute reservation timeout is measured from checkout initiation, not from the last user interaction","'Delivery date' for refund window purposes is a single recorded timestamp, not a range","Zero inventory means zero available (not zero total — reserved units are already subtracted from available)","The discount mutual exclusion applies at the order level as stated, not per line item"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec states discount codes cannot combine with promotional pricing 'per order,' but does not clarify behavior for mixed carts (some items on promotion, others not). Does one promoted item block discount codes for the entire order, or only for that line item?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Merchandising","Finance"],"options":["A) Per-order: if any item has a promotion, no discount code can be applied to the order","B) Per-line-item: discount code applies only to non-promoted items, promoted items keep their promotional price"],"recommendation":"Option A (per-order) is safer and simpler to implement, but Option B is more customer-friendly. Recommend clarifying with the product team before implementation."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"When a multi-item cart has some items in stock and some out of stock at checkout, should the entire checkout be blocked or should partial checkout be allowed for available items only?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Fulfillment"],"options":["A) Atomic: entire checkout fails if any item is unavailable","B) Partial: out-of-stock items are removed, remaining items proceed"],"recommendation":"Option A (atomic) is consistent with the spec's language ('a product with zero inventory cannot be purchased') and avoids surprising the customer with a modified order. Recommend Option A with clear error messaging identifying the out-of-stock items."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
